title: 牙医谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\203-牙医谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Hercule Poirot and Inspector Japp interview Miss Meabelle Seale, a former actress and missionary who recently moved from India to the Glengowan Court Hotel. During the questioning, Japp asks if Mr. Morley mentioned any upsetting encounters, but she denies this. Miss Seale provides an extensive personal history, detailing her career in theater, her failed marriage, and her current work teaching public speaking. When Japp suggests she may be required to testify in court, she accepts the prospect with enthusiasm.

Following the interview, Japp and Poirot travel to the Savoy Hotel to speak with Mr. Ambrosio, but a hotel employee informs them that Ambrosio died only half an hour prior. 

Twenty-four hours later, Japp calls Poirot to report that he believes the case is solved. Based on an autopsy report, Japp concludes that Morley's death was a suicide resulting from a medical error. Japp posits that Morley accidentally administered a lethal overdose of adrenaline and procaine to Ambrosio and, unable to face the professional ruin and guilt, shot himself.

Poirot remains skeptical, questioning why a professional would commit suicide without leaving a note and doubting that a medical mistake would lead to such an extreme reaction. Japp presents alternative theories, noting Ambrosio's suspicious past involving intelligence work and blackmail in Europe and India. Japp suggests that Ambrosio might have blackmailed Morley, or that Ambrosio killed Morley and then committed suicide using stolen drugs. However, Japp maintains his conviction that Morley's death was a tragic accident caused by overwork.
